section: JEF P101 – A Triumph of Surgery
title: A Triumph of Surgery – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: James Herriot

questions.question: Who is Tricki?
questions.correct_answer: A small dog owned by Mrs Pumphrey
questions.explanation: Tricki is the pet dog whose illness and recovery form the central theme of the prose.

questions.question: Why was Mrs Pumphrey worried about Tricki?
questions.correct_answer: He had become very fat and inactive
questions.explanation: Tricki was listless, obese, and lacked energy due to overfeeding.

questions.question: What was the main reason for Tricki’s illness?
questions.explanation: Mrs Pumphrey gave Tricki rich food without proper physical activity.

questions.question: Who is the narrator of the story?
questions.explanation: The story is narrated by the vet who treats Tricki.

questions.question: What treatment did the vet give Tricki?
questions.correct_answer: Rest, exercise, and a controlled diet
questions.explanation: No medicines or surgery were used; discipline and routine cured Tricki.

questions.question: Why is the title 'A Triumph of Surgery' ironic?
questions.explanation: Tricki recovered naturally, yet Mrs Pumphrey believed it was a medical miracle.

questions.question: How did Tricki change after treatment?
questions.correct_answer: He became active, healthy, and energetic
questions.explanation: Exercise and proper food transformed Tricki into a fit dog.
questions.source_section: Middle to end of the prose

questions.question: What quality of the vet helps Tricki recover?
questions.explanation: The vet’s practical approach ensures Tricki’s recovery.

questions.question: What message does the prose convey?
questions.correct_answer: Excessive love without discipline can be harmful
questions.explanation: True care requires responsibility and moderation.

questions.question: Why did Mrs Pumphrey think Tricki’s recovery was a miracle?
questions.correct_answer: She believed surgery had cured him
questions.explanation: She misunderstood the simple natural treatment as advanced medical care.

notes.title: Overindulgence
notes.content: The story highlights how excessive affection and rich food can damage health.

notes.title: Role of Discipline
notes.content: Proper routine, exercise, and diet restore Tricki’s health.

facts.fact: No medicines or surgery were used to cure Tricki.
The story uses humour to convey a serious message.
